aiashley — global digest Generated 08:15 UTC

Friday, July 24, 2026

International News · 16 Sources · 4 Regions

Daily Brief

The global trade system faces renewed strain as the United States imposed 10-12.5% tariffs on 60 trading partners, citing forced labor concerns that trading partners and experts dismiss as pretextual [BBC, RNZ, Globe and Mail]. Energy markets are in turmoil with Brent crude surpassing $100 per barrel for the first time since May, driven by escalating US-Iran hostilities and Houthi attacks on Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea [BBC, France 24, Al Jazeera]. The EU condemned the tanker attacks as 'inflammatory' while the US launched its 13th consecutive night of strikes on Iran [RFI, The Hindu]. In Europe, deadly wildfires have forced the evacuation of over 40,000 people in southwest France, with Spain declaring a national emergency as fires threaten communities near Madrid [BBC, France 24, The Hindu]. Ukraine's military leadership underwent dramatic upheaval as ousted Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov refused alternative positions and demanded reinstatement, while 43-year-old Mykhailo Drapatyi was named the new commander-in-chief following protests that forced out his predecessor [BBC, Al Jazeera]. President Trump complicated the newly announced US-Saudi nuclear deal by publicly conditioning it on Saudi recognition of Israel, a stance Riyadh has historically resisted [BBC, The Hindu].

Top Stories
01 Economics

US Imposes New Tariffs on 60 Trading Partners, Citing Forced Labor Claims

  • The United States imposed tariffs of 10-12.5% on 60 trading partners including the UK, EU, China, Canada, Australia, and New Zealand, covering 99.4% of US imports
  • The new tariffs replace an identical levy expiring Friday, following the Supreme Court's February ruling that Trump's original 'Liberation Day' tariffs were illegally enacted under emergency powers
  • US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer claimed the tariffs address forced labor in global supply chains, but trade experts and targeted countries rejected this justification as pretextual
  • Canada faces 10% tariffs with exemptions for USMCA goods and all oil and gas; New Zealand and Australia face 12.5% rates
Coverage
BBC WorldQuoted expert Caroline Freund stating Trump is 'looking for a legal reason to put the tariffs in' RNZEmphasized New Zealand's rejection of forced labor claims and minister's view that US is 'looking for any means' to impose tariffs Globe and MailFocused on Canadian exemptions and characterized move as part of 'continuing quest' for protectionist measures Nikkei AsiaHighlighted Asian governments' disappointment and skepticism about the legal basis
02 Conflict

Oil Prices Surge Past $100 as Houthis Attack Saudi Tankers Amid US-Iran Escalation

  • Brent crude rose more than 6% to exceed $100 per barrel for the first time since May after Houthi rebels attacked Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea
  • The attacks threaten a key export route Saudi Arabia has used to bypass the Strait of Hormuz, which Iran has effectively closed amid ongoing hostilities
  • The US completed its 13th consecutive night of strikes on Iran, with Trump threatening 'major military punishment' and considering using frozen Iranian assets to pay for shipping damages
  • The EU's foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas condemned the tanker attack as 'unacceptable, illegal and further inflames an already volatile situation'
Coverage
BBC WorldEmphasized economic impact on UK consumers and inflation concerns France 24Provided detailed live coverage of US strikes on Iranian military targets RFI EnglishLed with EU diplomatic response and condemnation Al JazeeraHighlighted Iranian Foreign Minister's condemnation of US asset seizure threats as 'incendiary precedent' The HinduIncluded Pakistan's warning of retaliation for any attacks on its vessels
03 Climate

Deadly Wildfires Force Mass Evacuations Across Southern Europe

  • Over 40,000 people have been evacuated in southwest France as wildfires spread across more than 10,000 hectares in the Gironde region, with the entire Cap-Ferret peninsula ordered to evacuate
  • Two French firefighters died earlier in the week; 32 fires are currently burning across France with over 50,000 hectares burned since January—nearly three times last year's total
  • Spain declared a national emergency as fires near Madrid and in Avila province forced more than 10,000 evacuations; a firefighter died in Sicily
  • French President Macron described the situation as 'very tense' and requested EU assistance; experts link worsening fire seasons to climate change
Coverage
BBC EuropeDetailed evacuation logistics including boat evacuations from peninsula France 24Provided live coverage with Interior Minister quotes on scale of fires RFI EnglishFocused on potential cause and prefect's response measures The HinduEmphasized climate change link and seasonal vegetation patterns
04 Conflict

Ukraine's Ousted Defense Minister Refuses Alternative Posts, Demands Reinstatement

  • Former Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov rejected President Zelensky's offer of deputy prime minister and other posts, insisting only three positions determine the war's course: president, defense minister, and commander-in-chief
  • Fedorov's July 15 dismissal triggered mass protests; Zelensky subsequently fired Commander-in-Chief Oleksandr Syrskyi on July 21 following conflicts between the two officials
  • Major General Mykhailo Drapatyi, 43, was named new commander-in-chief; he gained fame in 2014 for driving through separatist barricades in Mariupol
  • Ukraine's armed forces have achieved their best performance in three years, with 193,500 Russian casualties in 2026 and net Russian territorial losses in July
Coverage
BBC EuropeEmphasized Fedorov's innovations and anti-corruption efforts; noted protesters demanding his reinstatement Al JazeeraProvided detailed analysis of Ukraine's military performance improvements under outgoing leadership The HinduCovered Lavrov-Rubio meeting context and Russian warnings on arms sales
05 Geopolitics

Trump Conditions US-Saudi Nuclear Deal on Kingdom's Recognition of Israel

  • President Trump announced that the civilian nuclear deal signed Wednesday with Saudi Arabia is 'totally subject to Saudi Arabia joining the Abraham Accords'
  • The condition was not mentioned in the deal text; White House said Trump had discussed this requirement with Saudi leaders in previous conversations
  • Saudi Arabia has not responded publicly; the kingdom has historically resisted normalization with Israel without a pathway to Palestinian statehood
  • Israeli PM Netanyahu called potential Saudi recognition a 'historic leap' while experts had already raised concerns about nuclear proliferation risks
Coverage
BBC WorldNoted uncertainty about whether condition was in deal text or separately communicated The HinduCharacterized the announcement as throwing the agreement 'into turmoil' RFI EnglishMentioned Colombia embassy move in context of Jerusalem diplomatic developments

  • Ukrainian drones strike five Wildberries warehouses across Russia in week-long campaign targeting logistics hubs BBC Europe, France 24, Globe and Mail
  • Germany approves controversial French-Russian nuclear fuel rod joint venture despite Ukraine war concerns RFI English
  • Zinedine Zidane set to be unveiled as France national team coach on July 28, replacing Didier Deschamps RFI English
  • Former Senegalese President Macky Sall pledges UN reform as six candidates debate ahead of Secretary-General selection RFI English
  • Indian activist Sonam Wangchuk ends 26-day hunger strike as 'Cockroach' youth protests continue demanding education minister's resignation Al Jazeera, Globe and Mail, Nikkei Asia

  • Indonesian court convicts 19 people in baby trafficking case involving at least 34 infants sold to Singapore BBC World
  • British army cancels Kenya training exercises amid dispute over legal jurisdiction for soldiers The Guardian
  • Japan Immigration Services Agency to hire over 200 enforcement officers in rare emergency staffing expansion Nikkei Asia
  • SK Group chairman ordered to pay record $643 million divorce settlement in South Korean court ruling Nikkei Asia
